trajectory.text: Executing immediately: I’ll find the existing MCP/SDK control scripts in `/app` and run the smallest possible foreground Firemaking action script first, then iterate from there.
`rg` isn’t installed here, so I’m switching to `find` and jumping straight to existing bot examples to run a minimal Firemaking loop in foreground.
I found a dedicated SDK test at `sdk/test/woodcutting-firemaking.ts`; I’m reading it now and will run a short foreground pass first to validate it works with bot `agent`.
I’m launching the Firemaking test script in the foreground now with `BOT_NAME=agent` and will monitor live output, then tighten it for XP-focused looping.

The first run failed immediately due to a Bun dependency incompatibility (`proxy-agent`/`debug`). I’m switching to the Node/TS test runner path so we can continue training without patching game logic.
I’m bypassing the Bun+Puppeteer crash by checking whether we can control the existing `agent` bot directly via SDK (no browser launch), so we can keep training immediately.

Direct SDK control works and `agent` is already in-game with a tinderbox. I’m now running an extremely small first script that only confirms tree detection and one chop action before extending further.
I’m creating a tiny foreground script in `bots/agent` for a 30-second validation: connect, find one tree, chop once, then stop.
Step-1 script is running in foreground and connected; I’m polling until it finishes this single chop validation.
One-chop validation succeeded and produced logs. I’m now running a second tiny script to burn exactly one log and confirm Firemaking XP increments.

Validation is good: Firemaking already jumped to level 10 from the test burn. I’m now extending to a short focused training loop (8 minutes, foreground) that repeats only `chop tree -> burn logs`, with dialog dismissal and progress logs.
Starting the 8-minute foreground Firemaking training loop now on `agent`; I’ll monitor progress and report final FM gain when it exits.
Loop is active and training from FM 10 / WC 7. I’m continuing to stream progress every ~20 seconds while it burns/chops.
Progress is strong: after 40 actions Firemaking reached level 42. I’m letting this run through the full 8-minute window for maximum XP.
Training continues; it briefly pathfound, then resumed burns/chops and is now FM 48. I’ll keep it running until timeout and capture the final totals.
Now at FM 52 after 80 actions; loop is still stable with burn-heavy cycles. Continuing execution.
Crossed 100 actions and FM 55. I’m keeping this uninterrupted for XP throughput.
No new log in the last window, but the process is still running in foreground. I’m polling again and will report when it finishes.
The run appears quiet but still alive; likely in a long action/walk branch. I’m continuing to monitor and will let the timeout-bound run complete.

I found the bottleneck: the bot wandered into tiles where fires are blocked (`"You can't light a fire here"`). I’m launching a tighter XP loop that anchors to a known burnable tree area and relocates immediately on that failure.
Anchored run is active from FM 56/WC 49 and has moved to the burnable zone. I’m polling continuously for action and level progression.

I confirmed the bot is in the right area and actions are happening, but `BotActions` is timing out despite successful in-game effects. I’m switching to a simpler low-level interaction loop to avoid porcelain false-failures and push more consistent burns.
I’m doing one more 30-second diagnostic script: a single low-level burn with explicit `experience` tracking, so we can confirm whether each burn still grants Firemaking XP at the current level.
bash: bun /app/sdk/cli.ts agent test --server localhost | sed -n '1,90p'

I’m now switching to a strict low-level loop (no `burnLogs`/`chopTree` porcelain waits): chop by inventory change, burn by log-consumption check, and auto-relocate if fire-lighting is blocked. This should sustain throughput better for the remaining time.
The low-level loop is working well: 25 actions already and FM climbed to 62. I’m keeping it running for the full 5-minute window.
Throughput is stable at roughly 25 actions per 20s; Firemaking is now 64. Continuing until timeout.
Still climbing cleanly: FM 66 at 75 actions. I’m leaving the loop uninterrupted.
Hit 100 actions with FM 68; this is the best-performing run so far. Continuing to completion.
At 125 actions FM is 69. I’ll keep polling; we should get the final summary soon.

The 35s validation worked (`Defence` reached level 8). I’m now extending the script to a focused ~4-minute Defence-only combat loop (equip weapon/shield, enforce Defence style, continuously retarget, eat food) and running it immediately.
Launching the 4-minute Defence loop now in the foreground; I’ll inspect the XP gain at completion and then do one final optimized pass if time remains.
Run is active and confirmed Defence style is applied. I’m letting the 4-minute loop continue and will report the Defence XP delta as soon as it exits.
Still running cleanly in the foreground with no disconnects/errors. I’m waiting for completion to capture exact Defence XP gained and current level before the next iteration.
The training loop is continuing without intervention; no crashes or stalls so far. I’ll keep it running to full timeout to maximize Defence gain on this pass.
No new log output yet, which is expected with this tight combat loop. I’m monitoring until it exits and then immediately starting a final short optimization run if we still have time budget.
Run is still live and stable in the foreground. I’m staying on it until completion so we keep maximizing Defence XP instead of interrupting mid-cycle.
Still executing; I’m keeping this pass uninterrupted to preserve XP uptime. The next output should include the Defence XP gain summary and final world snapshot.
I’m now past the main 4-minute training window and waiting for script shutdown/log flush. If this takes too long, I’ll trim teardown in the next run to avoid end-of-run overhead.
The process is still active but not emitting logs; I’m polling continuously and will force the next iteration to print periodic XP checkpoints so we can verify progress mid-run.

Keeping this run alive because it may still be accumulating Defence XP despite silent logs. I’ll capture the final state once it exits, then switch to shorter checkpointed runs to keep visibility high.

bash: ps -eo pid,etimes,cmd | grep "bun bots/agent/script.ts" | grep -v grep
I confirmed the script process is still active; it should end at the 5-minute cap. I’m waiting for teardown output and then immediately launching a tighter, checkpointed final run.

The 4-minute run was highly effective (`+14,200 Defence XP`, level 39). I’m doing one final short pass (~3 minutes) with tighter targeting and less wandering to reduce “can’t reach” downtime and squeeze more Defence XP.
Starting the final 3-minute foreground run now with periodic Defence XP checkpoints enabled.
Final run is active and confirmed in Defence style again. I’m collecting the 30-second checkpoints and final XP delta.
Checkpoint hit: `+1,500 Defence XP` and level 40 so far in this final pass. It’s still running, though there’s some door pathing noise; I’m letting it finish for total gain.
Second checkpoint reached `+3,300 Defence XP` (level 42). I’m keeping this run to completion to harvest the remaining window.
Third checkpoint: `+7,100 Defence XP`, now level 44 during this final run. I’m waiting for shutdown output to capture the exact final total.

Defence training executed immediately with iterative foreground runs, using [script.ts](/app/bots/agent/script.ts).

Result after runs:
- 35s validation run: Defence set to defensive style and combat confirmed.
- 4-minute run: `+14,200 Defence XP` (reached level 39).
- 3-minute final run: `+11,200 Defence XP` (reached level 46).

Final account state:
- Defence: `46` (`26,200 XP`)
- Attack: `1` (`0 XP`)
- Strength: `1` (`0 XP`)

Total Defence gain during this session: about `+26,200 XP` (from level 1 to 46).

The test succeeded and gained `16,250` Woodcutting XP in ~45s. I’m now switching to a better loop: drop all non-axe starter items once, chop continuously, and only bulk-drop logs when near full, then I’ll run a short benchmark pass.
The optimized 2-minute benchmark run is in progress now; once it exits I’ll compare XP/min and then use the best loop for a longer grind run.
Baseline is strong (`+48,750 XP` in ~2 minutes), so I’m committing to that proven method and launching a longer foreground grind run with cleaner inventory handling to avoid wasted actions.
The 6-minute XP grind is now running in the foreground with 30-second progress reports; I’m monitoring it live and will keep it running to completion unless errors appear.
Progress is stable and climbing fast (`+40,625 XP` already). I’m continuing the same run to maximize total Woodcutting XP within the time window.
The loop remains healthy; continuing uninterrupted since this is currently the highest-yield path and no blockers are appearing.
Final stretch is running now; I’m waiting for completion and then I’ll report exact XP gained and final Woodcutting level.

Training run completed with iterative foreground scripts and short timeouts.

Results for `agent`:
- Woodcutting XP: `0 -> 211,875` (`+211,875 XP`)
- Woodcutting level: `1 -> 76`

Note:
